title: olearia decurrens
intro: Olearia decurrens, also known as the 'native bush daisy' or 'swamp daisy', is a shrub native to eastern Australia, characterised by its white or pink flowers and silvery-green leaves. The leaves and flowers of Olearia decurrens have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been reported to have potential medicinal uses. The health benefits of Olearia decurrens include relief from colds, fever, and skin irritations, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Olearia decurrens can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed orally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
